prompt_text: Assessment of diabetic retinopathy using two-field 60 degrees fundus photography. A comparison between red-free, black-and-white prints and colour transparencies. To assess the severity of diabetic retinopathy and maculopathy by comparing two different photographic methods; two-field 60 degrees photography using red-free, black-and-white prints and colour transparencies. Two ophthalmologists independently graded the photographs of 74 patients using grading scales for retinopathy and maculopathy designed for 60 degrees photography.

prompt_text: Waste is generally stored in large, wheeled metal bins referred to as “dumpsters” which are designed to be emptied mechanically by specially equipped garbage trucks having hydraulically-operated lifting arms or forks which engage, lift and tip the dumpster. Trash bins have one or more hinged lids that can be opened to deposit trash into the bin. The bins are designed to be emptied by a trash truck.
base_text: Waste is generally stored in large, wheeled metal bins referred to as “dumpsters” which are designed to be emptied mechanically by specially equipped garbage trucks having hydraulically-operated lifting arms or forks which engage, lift and tip the dumpster. Trash bins have one or more hinged lids that can be opened to deposit trash into the bin. The bins are designed to be emptied by a trash truck. These bins are typically constructed from durable materials such as steel or heavy-duty plastic to withstand outdoor conditions and repeated use. In some cases, dumpsters are equipped with internal liners or bags to contain waste and prevent leakage. Modern waste management systems often incorporate compactor bins, which use mechanical compression to reduce the volume of waste before it is lifted by the truck, optimizing space and reducing the frequency of pickups.
final_text: Waste is generally stored in large, wheeled metal bins referred to as “dumpsters” which are designed to be emptied mechanicakly by specially equipped garbagE trucks having hydraulically-operated lifting arms or forks which engage, lift and tip the dumpster. Modern waste management systems often incorporate compactor bins, which use mechanical compression to reduce the volume of waste before it is lifted by the truck, optimizing space and reducing the frequency of pickups.

label: ai
prompt_text: In automotive OEM finishing, the interior parts of the automobile bodies are normally first painted by pneumatic spraying or compressed air spraying. Pneumatic spraying is selected because the cavities and recesses are difficult to coat electrostatically, owing to the formation of Faraday cages.
base_text: In automotive OEM finishing, the interior parts of the automobile bodies are normally first painted by pneumatic spraying or compressed air spraying. Pneumatic spraying is selected because the cavities and recesses are difficult to coat electrostatically, owing to the formation of Faraday cages. This method allows for better coverage in complex geometries, although it may result in higher paint consumption and overspray compared to electrostatic techniques.
final_text: In automotive OEM finishing, the interior parts of the automobile bodies are normally first painted by pneumatic spraying or compressed air spraying. Pneumatic spraying is selected because the cavities and recesses are difficult to coat electrostatically, owing to the formation of Faraday cages. This method allows for better coverage in complex geometries, although it may result in higher paint consumption and overspray compared to electrostatic techniques.

label: ai
prompt_text: This invention relates generally to improvements in plasma-arc torches and, particularly, to a circuit and method for ensuring that the parts of a contact start plasma-arc torch are properly in place before allowing the output voltage to ramp up to its final value. Plasma-arc torches, also known as electric arc torches, are commonly used for cutting, welding, and spray bonding. Such torches typically operate by directing a plasma consisting of ionized gas particles toward a workpiece. In general, a pressurized gas to be ionized is supplied to the front end of the torch and flows past an electrode before exiting through an orifice in the torch tip.
base_text: This invention relates generally to improvements in plasma-arc torches and, particularly, to a circuit and method for ensuring that the parts of a contact start plasma-arc torch are properly in place before allowing the output voltage to ramp up to its final value. Plasma-arc torches, also known as electric arc torches, are commonly used for cutting, welding, and spray bonding. Such torches typically operate by directing a plasma consisting of ionized gas particles toward a workpiece. In general, a pressurized gas to be ionized is supplied to the front end of the torch and flows past an electrode before exiting through an orifice in the torch tip. The proper alignment and securement of these components are critical to prevent arcing, overheating, or damage to the torch and workpiece.
final_text: This invention relates generally to improvements in plasma-arc torches and, particularly, to a circuit and method for ensuring that the parts of a contact start plasma-arc torch are properly in place sefore allowing the output voltage to ramp up to its final value. Plasma-arc torches, also known as electric arc torches, are commonly used for cutting, welding, and spray bonding. Such torches typically operate by directing a plasma consisting of ionized gas particles toward a workpiece. In general, a pressurized gas to be ionized is supplied to the front end of the torch and flows past an electrode before exiting through an orifice in the torch tip. The proper alignment and securement of these components are critical to prevent arcing, overheating, or damage to the torch and workpiece.

1. Field of the Invention
The present invention relates to a technique of transmitting audio information via a wide area network.
base_text:
1. Field of the Invention
The present invention relates to a technique of transmitting audio information via a wide area network. 2. Background of the Invention  
Conventional methods of transmitting audio over wide area networks often suffer from latency, bandwidth inefficiency, and degradation of sound quality due to compression artifacts. These limitations are particularly pronounced in real-time applications such as voice over IP (VoIP), video conferencing, and live streaming, where low delay and high fidelity are critical.
final_text:

base_text:
J. Holiday

Nahom Thorton Grymes (born November 29, 1984), better known by his stage name J. Holiday, is an American singer, songwriter, and record producer. He is best known for his work as a member of the R&B group Pretty Tony, and for his solo career, which includes the Billboard Hot 100 charting single "Want You Back".
final_text:
J. Holiday

prompt_text:
---
abstract: |
 The concept of broken symmetry is used to study bifurcations of equilibria and dynamical instabilities in dynamic model of one-mode laser (nonresonant complex Lorenz model) on the basis of modified Hopf theory. It is shown that an invariant set of stationary points bifurcates into an invariant torus (doubly-periodic branching solution). Influence of the symmetry breaking on stability of branching solutions is investigated as a function of detuning. The invariant torus is found to be stable under the detuning exceeds its critical value, so that dynamically broken symmetry results in the appearance of low frequency Goldstone-type mode.
base_text:
---

prompt_text: Biophysical characterisation of the persistent sodium current of the Nav1.6 neuronal sodium channel: a single-channel analysis. Na(v)1.6 is the major voltage-gated sodium channel at nodes of Ranvier. This channel has been shown to produce a robust persistent inward current in whole-cell experiments. Na(v)1.6 plays an important role in axonal conduction and may significantly contribute to the pathophysiology of the injured nervous system through this persistent current.

label: ai
prompt_text: [Hemostatic properties of gastric juice under basal conditions and after stimulation with pentagastrin or secretin-pancreozymin (author's transl)]. Unsoluble gastric mucus and gastric juice from 11 normal volunteers were separated by centrifugation and investigated for hemostatic properties. Unsoluble gastric mucus added to citrated blood shortened reaction time and increased duration of maximum amplitude of thrombelastographic recordings, indicating an acceleration of hemostatic processes. On the other hand gastric juice inhibited coagulation and enhanced fibrinolytic activities, as shown by thrombelastographic recordings, prothrombin time, partial thromboplastin time, and euglobulin lysis time.
